div1.entryFree: Thereupon, on this; in consequence of this; therefore (always referring to sentences): and t. I drew my sword on you, Err. V, 262. and t. these errors are arose, Err. V, 262 and t. I will kiss thee, Ado V, 2, 50. and t. thou speakest the fairest shoot, LLL IV, 1, 12. and t. I drink unto your grace, H4B IV, 2, 68. and t. give me your daughter, H5 V, 2, 375. and t. he sends you this good news, R3 III, 2, 48. I dare t. pawn the moiety of my estate to your ring, Cymb. I, 4, 118.
